claims:
BIKTARVY®: robust and durable efficacy through 5 years in treatment-naïve people with HIV*1
* At Week 48 in pooled data from treatment-naïve participants: efficacy was 91% for BIKTARVY® (pooled [n=634]) vs 93% (ABC/3TC/DTG [n=315]) in Study 1489 (95% CI: -2.1 [-5.9 to 1.6]) and 93% (FTC/TAF + DTG [n=325]) in Study 1490 (95% CI: -1.9 [-5.6 to 1.8]).2
Primary endpoint: at Week 48, pooled data from treatment-naïve participants, efficacy was 91% for BIKTARVY® (pooled n=634)*+2
99% pooled efficacy (M=E) through Week 240 (n=426/432)+1
67% pooled efficacy (M=F) through Week 240 (n=426/634)+1

claims:
BIKTARVY®: forgiveness* demonstrated in Phase 3 clinical trials†1
BIKTARVY® maintained higher virologic suppression through 144 weeks in treatment-naïve participants with <85% adherence vs DTG + 2 NRTI regimens‡ (p=0.03)+1

components.content: BIKTARVY bictegravir 50mg/emtricitabine 200mg/ tenofovir alafenamide 25mg tablets Three inverted pyramid shapes are stacked on top of each other, with the largest red, the middle teal, and the smallest dark blue, forming a stylized upward-pointing arrow or growth symbol
components.name: BIKTARVY bictegravir 50mg/emtricitabine 200mg/ tenofovir alafenamide 25mg tablets Three inverted pyr

components.content: Bar chart: Efficacy (HIV <50 copies/mL) of BIKTARVY® vs DTG + 2 NRTI regimens stratified by adherence level in a pooled, retrospective analysis of 1489 and 1490+ Y-axis: Participants (%) X-axis: Adherence category and treatment regimen Data Series: BIKTARVY® (n=623) - Red bars - High (adherence ≥95%, n=436): 99% - Intermediate (adherence ≥85-<95%, n=149): 99% - Low (adherence <85%, n=38): 97% DTG + 2 NRTIs‡ (n=637) - Dark gray bars (Week 144) - High (adherence ≥95%, n=436): 98% - Intermediate (adherence ≥85-<95%, n=149): 96% - Low (adherence <85%, n=38): 82% P-values: - Between BIKTARVY® High and Intermediate adherence: p=0.45 - Between BIKTARVY® Intermediate and Low adherence: p=0.50 - Between DTG + 2 NRTIs High and Intermediate adherence: p<0.0001 - Between DTG + 2 NRTIs Intermediate and Low adherence: p=0.005
components.name: Bar chart: Efficacy (HIV <50 copies/mL) of BIKTARVY® vs DTG + 2 NRTI regimens stratified by adherenc

content: BIKTARVY® shows superior virologic suppression in treatment-naïve patients with low adherence over 144 weeks.

footnotes.content: Forgiveness is defined as the ability of a given regimen to achieve and maintain virological suppression despite imperfect adherence.
‡ DTG + 2 NRTI regimens included: Study 1489, ABC/3TC/DTG (n=314) and Study 1490, DTG + FTC/TAF (n=323).

† A retrospective analysis evaluated treatment adherence and its effect on virologic outcomes in double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical studies in treatment-naïve (1489, 1490, 4458) or virologically suppressed (1844 and 4030) participants.¹ Participants with ≥1 returned pill bottle and ≥1 on-treatment HIV-1 RNA measurement were included in the analysis.¹ Adherence was measured by the number of pills taken divided by the number of pills prescribed. Assessment was limited to returned pill bottles.¹

claims:
BIKTARVY®: sustained efficacy through 3 years in virologically suppressed people with HIV*1,2
Through Week 48, in Studies 1844 (n=282) and 1878 (n=290), efficacy was 92-94% with BIKTARVY®*3
≥96% efficacy (M=E) maintained through the open-label extensions of both studies‡1,2
* At Week 48, efficacy was 94% for BIKTARVY® (n=282) vs 95% for ABC/3TC/DTG (n=281) in Study 1844 (95% CI: -1.4 [-5.5 to 2.6]) and 92% for BIKTARVY® (n=290) vs 89% for ABC/3TC or FTC/TDF plus boosted ATV or DRV (n=287) in Study 1878 (95% CI: 3.2 [-1.6 to 8.2]).3
content: BIKTARVY® shows sustained efficacy of 92-94% over three years in HIV patients.

footnotes.content: At Week 48, efficacy was 94% for BIKTARVY® (n=282) vs 95% for ABC/3TC/DTG (n=281) in Study 1844 (95% CI: -1.4 [-5.5 to 2.6]) and 92% for BIKTARVY® (n=290) vs 89% for ABC/3TC or FTC/TDF plus boosted ATV or DRV (n=287) in Study 1878 (95% CI: 3.2 [-1.6 to 8.2]).
+ Includes all participants treated with BIKTARVY® (Study 1844: n=547; Study 1878: n=534) in either the randomisation phases or the open-label extension phases of Studies 1844 and 1878.1.2

content: BIKTARVY® shows sustained efficacy and no treatment-emergent resistance over five years.

footnotes.content: * At Week 48 (primary endpoint) in pooled data from treatment-naïve participants: efficacy was 91% for BIKTARVY® (n=634) vs 93% (ABC/3TC/DTG [n=315]) in Study 1489 (95% CI: -2.1 [-5.9 to 1.6]) and 93% (FTC/TAF + DTG [n=325]) in Study 1490 (95% CI: -1.9 [-5.6 to 1.8]).¹ Efficacy defined as viral load <50 copies/mL. In a pooled analysis of both studies in participants initially randomised to BIKTARVY® (n=634), efficacy was 98.6% (M=E) at Week 240 with 0 treatment-emergent resistance in the final resistance analysis population.²

claims:
Treatment-naïve adults: Studies 1489 and 1490¹⁻⁵
Studies 1489 and 1490 were Phase 3, randomised, double- blind, multicentre clinical trials⁵
Primary endpoint:, - HIV-1 RNA <50 copies/mL at Week 48*
Secondary endpoints:²,´ - HIV-1 RNA <50 copies/mL at Weeks 96, 144, 192 and 240
OLE objectives:¹ - To evaluate efficacy and safety after additional exposure to BIKTARVY®
Randomised double-blind Study 1490: ART-r - Chronic HBV or infection allowed - eGFRCG ≥30 mL/ - No known resist - FTC, TAF, ABC, - HIV-1 RNA ≥500

components.content: Study Design: 1. **Randomization (1:1)**: * **Arm 1 (n=320)**: BIKTARVY® QD * **Arm 2 (n=325)**: FTC/TAF + DTG QD 2. **Timeline**: * **Blinded treatment (Week 0-144)**: * Week 0 * Week 48: Primary endpoint * Week 96: Secondary endpoint * Week 144: Secondary endpoint * **Open-label extension (Week 144-240)**: * From Week 144, Arm 2 switches to BIKTARVY® QD. * Week 192: Secondary endpoint * Week 240: Secondary endpoint
components.name: Study Design: 1. **Randomization (1:1)**: * **Arm 1 (n=320)**: BIKTARVY® QD * **Arm 2 (n=325)**: FTC

components.content: This flowchart outlines the design of Study 1489 for ART-naïve adults, which is a randomised double-blind study. The initial population is defined by the following criteria: - HLA B*5701 negative - Negative for chronic HBV - eGFRCG ≥50 mL/min - No known resistance to FTC, TAF, ABC, or 3TC - HIV-1 RNA ≥500 copies/mL This population is randomised 1:1 into two main groups: - Group 1 (n=314) receives BIKTARVY® QD. - Group 2 (n=315) is further divided or compared, with one arm receiving ABC/3TC/DTG QD (indicated by a gray box) and another arm receiving BIKTARVY® QD (indicated by a red box).
components.name: This flowchart outlines the design of Study 1489 for ART-naïve adults, which is a randomised double-

content: Phase 3 trials evaluated BIKTARVY® efficacy and safety in treatment-naïve adults over 240 weeks.
